BacktestRunRequest.description: POST /api/backtests/run body — kicks off a synchronous engine run.
BacktestRunRequest.title: BacktestRunRequest

BacktestRunTagsUpdate.description: PUT /api/backtests/{id}/tags body. Replaces the full tag list.
BacktestRunTagsUpdate.title: BacktestRunTagsUpdate

BacktestRunUpdate.description: PATCH /api/backtests/{id} body. Send `null` to clear the name.
BacktestRunUpdate.title: BacktestRunUpdate

ChatMessageRead.description: One message rendered in the chat thread.
ChatMessageRead.title: ChatMessageRead

ChatTurnRequest.description: POST body for a new chat turn.
ChatTurnRequest.title: ChatTurnRequest

ChatTurnResponse.description: Returns both new messages so the FE can append in order.
ChatTurnResponse.title: ChatTurnResponse

DatasetScanResult.description: POST /api/datasets/scan response — summary of what changed.

removed.description: rows for files that no longer exist on disk
removed.title: Removed

DatasetScanResult.title: DatasetScanResult

DriftComparisonRead.description: Full drift comparison for a strategy version.
DriftComparisonRead.title: DriftComparisonRead

DriftResultRead.description:
One drift signal computation result.

`signal_type` enumerates which signal this is (currently `win_rate` or
`entry_time`); each has its own interpretation of `live_value`,
`baseline_value`, and `deviation`. See `app.services.drift_comparison`
for the formulas.

`status` is a tri-color summary: OK / WATCH / WARN. `incomplete` flags
when the sample size was below the threshold for a reliable read —
callers should display the result with a "tentative" hint.
DriftResultRead.title: DriftResultRead

ExperimentUpdate.description: PATCH /api/experiments/{id} body. Only fields present are applied.
ExperimentUpdate.title: ExperimentUpdate

FirmRuleProfileCreate.description:
POST body for a brand-new (custom) firm profile. The simulator's
8 sim-relevant fields are required; everything else has a sensible
default.
FirmRuleProfileCreate.title: FirmRuleProfileCreate

FirmRuleProfilePatch.description:
PATCH body — every field optional. Pydantic v2 distinguishes
"field omitted" from "field set to null" via `model_dump(
exclude_unset=True)`, which the endpoint relies on for partial
updates. Setting `verification_status="verified"` stamps
`verified_at = now()`; editing any rule field while currently
verified flips the status back to unverified.
FirmRuleProfilePatch.title: FirmRuleProfilePatch

FirmRuleProfileRead.description: Full editable firm rule profile — what /profiles endpoints return.
FirmRuleProfileRead.title: FirmRuleProfileRead

IngesterStatus.description:
Live ingester heartbeat — mirror of {DATA_ROOT}/heartbeat/live_ingester.json.

Written by app.ingest.live every HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SEC. Read by
GET /api/monitor/ingester so the UI can show live feed health.

`data_schema` is aliased to "schema" for both input and output so the
on-disk JSON key stays "schema" without shadowing BaseModel.schema().
IngesterStatus.title: IngesterStatus

LiveSignalRead.description: One row from `live_signals` — what the live bot emitted.
LiveSignalRead.title: LiveSignalRead

LiveTradesPipelineStatus.description:
Health snapshot of the daily live-trades import pipeline.

Composed from three local sources on benpc:
- latest `BacktestRun(source="live")` row (what the importer last wrote)
- the JSONL in the Taildrop inbox (what the next import will read)
- the import log tail (what the scheduled task did on its last fire)
LiveTradesPipelineStatus.title: LiveTradesPipelineStatus

NoteTypesRead.description:
GET /api/notes/types body. Surfaces NOTE_TYPES vocabulary so the
frontend stays driven by the backend.
NoteTypesRead.title: NoteTypesRead

NoteUpdate.description:
PATCH /api/notes/{id} body. Only fields present in the request
are applied; omit a field to leave it untouched. Cannot move a note
between attachments — delete and recreate for that.
NoteUpdate.title: NoteUpdate

PromptGenerateResponse.description:
POST /api/prompts/generate response.

`prompt_text` is the full markdown blob ready to paste.
`bundled_context_summary` lists which sections were included so the
UI can show the user what got packaged (e.g., "3 versions, 12 notes,
2 experiments, latest run, autopsy"). It's diagnostic, not load-bearing.
PromptGenerateResponse.title: PromptGenerateResponse

PropFirmConfigIn.description:
POST body. Either pass `preset_key` (with optional overrides) or fully
specify every field.
PropFirmConfigIn.title: PropFirmConfigIn

ReplayBar.description: One OHLCV bar in the replay timeline. ts is ISO-8601 UTC.
ReplayBar.title: ReplayBar

ReplayEntry.description:
One entry/exit pair from a backtest run, anchored to the
corresponding bars on the timeline.

`entry_ts` and `exit_ts` are timestamps the chart can use to draw
markers at the right candle. `pnl` is in dollars, `r_multiple` if
available; both are nullable because some imports leave them blank.
ReplayEntry.title: ReplayEntry

ReplayFvgZone.description:
One Fair Value Gap zone detected on the replay's resampled 5m
candles. Frontend renders these as semi-transparent price-range
bands so a reviewer can see "did this trade enter inside an FVG?"
ReplayFvgZone.title: ReplayFvgZone

ReplayPayload.description: Full payload for one (symbol, date) replay request.
ReplayPayload.title: ReplayPayload

RiskEvaluationRead.description: Result of applying a profile to a backtest run.
RiskEvaluationRead.title: RiskEvaluationRead

RiskProfileRead.description:
Profile as returned to the client.

`allowed_hours` is parsed back from `RiskProfile.allowed_hours_json`
in the API endpoint before serialization. `strategy_params` is the
dict of default strategy params this profile prefills on the
Run-a-Backtest form (None = no opinion).
RiskProfileRead.title: RiskProfileRead

RiskProfileStatusesRead.description: Vocabulary endpoint payload — mirrors STRATEGY_STAGES exposure.
RiskProfileStatusesRead.title: RiskProfileStatusesRead

RiskProfileUpdate.description: PATCH body — fields are optional; endpoint applies only those set.
RiskProfileUpdate.title: RiskProfileUpdate

ScheduledTaskStatus.description:
One Windows scheduled task's last/next run + result.

On non-Windows hosts, the task list is returned empty and
`platform_supported=False` so the frontend shows a clear empty
state rather than implying everything is broken.
ScheduledTaskStatus.title: ScheduledTaskStatus

SimulationRunConfigOut.description: Echo of the request shape for the run-detail page's config panel.
SimulationRunConfigOut.title: SimulationRunConfigOut

StrategyDefinitionRead.description: One runnable strategy + the metadata the form needs to render.
StrategyDefinitionRead.title: StrategyDefinitionRead

StrategyParamFieldSchema.description:
One field of a strategy's params, in a frontend-friendly shape.

`type` mirrors JSON Schema's primitive types we actually use today;
`label` is the form-row title; `min`/`max`/`step` are optional UI
hints for number inputs; `description` is a one-line hint shown
under the field. `enum` lets a strategy expose dropdown choices in
the future (unused so far).

`group` lets the inline runner bucket fields by concern:
  - `signal`  : algorithmic knobs that define what the strategy detects
  - `risk`    : per-trade risk caps (stop, target, max risk pts/$)
  - `session` : portfolio-level limits (max trades/day, dedup window,
    entry-window hours)
Untagged fields fall into the `signal` group by default.
StrategyParamFieldSchema.title: StrategyParamFieldSchema

StrategyStagesRead.description:
GET /api/strategies/stages body. Exposes the lifecycle vocabulary so
the frontend pipeline board column order is driven by the backend.
StrategyStagesRead.title: StrategyStagesRead

StrategyUpdate.description:
PATCH /api/strategies/{id} body. Only fields present in the request
are applied; omit a field to leave it untouched.
StrategyUpdate.title: StrategyUpdate

StrategyVersionBaselineUpdate.description:
Body for PATCH /strategy-versions/{id}/baseline.

`run_id=None` clears the baseline. Otherwise the referenced run must
exist and must NOT be source="live" — comparing live against itself
is meaningless.
StrategyVersionBaselineUpdate.title: StrategyVersionBaselineUpdate

SystemSettingsRead.description:
Read-only inspection of how the app is currently configured.

Everything here is derived from process state at request time —
no DB persistence yet. When editable settings ship, this stays as
the "system facts" view; new endpoints will handle the user prefs.
SystemSettingsRead.title: SystemSettingsRead

TradeReplayAnchor.description: The actual trade being replayed — drawn as fixed lines on the chart.
TradeReplayAnchor.title: TradeReplayAnchor

TradeReplayRunRead.description: A live backtest run with its trades, for the /trade-replay picker.
TradeReplayRunRead.title: TradeReplayRunRead

TradeReplayTickRead.description:
One TBBO tick payload sent to the chart.

`ts` is the event time (`ts_event`). Trade-print fields are nullable
since only `action="T"` rows carry a trade size + side; quote-only
rows still update bid/ask.
TradeReplayTickRead.title: TradeReplayTickRead

TradeReplayTradeRead.description:
One trade in the picker.

`tbbo_available` is the gate — true if the TBBO partition for this
trade's entry date exists on disk. Trades without TBBO render as
disabled rows in the picker.
TradeReplayTradeRead.title: TradeReplayTradeRead

TradeReplayWindowRead.description: Windowed TBBO payload for one anchored trade.
TradeReplayWindowRead.title: TradeReplayWindowRead

WarehouseSchemaSummary.description:
Per-schema rollup of what's in the on-disk warehouse.

Schema = "tbbo" | "mbp-1" | "ohlcv-1m" | "ohlcv-1s" etc.
Counts are derived from the `datasets` table (populated by
`dataset_scanner.scan_datasets`), so a stale scan can show stale
counts — `WarehouseSummary.last_scan_ts` exposes the freshness.
WarehouseSchemaSummary.title: WarehouseSchemaSummary

schema.title: Response List Backtests Api Backtests Get

post.description:
Kick off a synchronous engine backtest. Loads bars, runs the strategy,
writes outputs to disk, persists the BacktestRun row, returns it.

get.description:
Strategies the engine resolver knows how to run, with their
parameter schemas (frontend-friendly JSON Schema-ish) so the
Run-a-Backtest form can render typed fields per strategy. The
list is hand-maintained in `app.services.strategy_registry` to
mirror `runner._resolve_strategy`.

schema.title: Response List Runnable Strategies Api Backtests Strategies Get

delete.description:
Delete a run and all its children.

ORM relationships are declared with cascade="all, delete-orphan", so
trades, equity_points, run_metrics, and config_snapshot go with it.
Notes keep a nullable FK and are not cascade-deleted — they survive
as floating research notes.

SQLite FK enforcement is off in this app (PRAGMA foreign_keys=0) and
the baseline_run_id columns were added via migrations that didn't
encode ON DELETE SET NULL, so we explicitly NULL out any
StrategyVersion.baseline_run_id and Experiment.baseline_run_id /
variant_run_id pointing at this run before deleting. Without this,
the Forward Drift Monitor's /drift/latest endpoint would render an
empty panel after the live-baseline run was deleted (observed
2026-04-28).

schema.title: Response List Backtest Equity Api Backtests  Backtest Id  Equity Get

put.description: Replace the full tag list on a run. Empty list clears all tags.

schema.title: Response List Backtest Trades Api Backtests  Backtest Id  Trades Get

get.description:
Snapshot of warehouse contents + scheduled-task health + disk space.

Cheap: scans one DB table, optionally calls Get-ScheduledTaskInfo
for each known task (Windows only, ~50ms × 4), and one disk-stat.
Frontend polls every ~30s.

schema.title: Response List Datasets Api Datasets Get

post.description:
Walk BS_DATA_ROOT and reconcile the datasets table against disk.

Files modified in the last 60s are skipped (in-progress writes).
Idempotent — running it twice in a row is a no-op the second time.

get.description:
List experiments, optionally filtered.

`strategy_id` filters across all versions of the strategy by joining
Experiment → StrategyVersion. `decision` is validated against
EXPERIMENT_DECISIONS.

schema.title: Response List Experiments Api Experiments Get

get.description: Decision vocabulary, mirrors STRATEGY_STAGES pattern.

Return the FEATURES registry as a flat list.

Each entry: { name, label, description, param_schema }. Frontend
renders one card per entry in the feature pantry; clicking "+ Add"
instantiates a `{feature: name, params: {}}` block in the strategy
spec.

schema.title: Response List Features Api Features Get

get.description:
Auto-resolve the most-recent live run's strategy_version and
return its drift comparison. Saves the frontend a round-trip.

404 if there are no live runs yet (frontend renders an empty state).
404 with a "no baseline" message if the resolved version has no
baseline assigned (frontend renders a "set a baseline at /strategies"
hint).

Compute Forward Drift Monitor signals for a strategy version.

Resolves the version's `baseline_run_id` and most-recent live run,
then runs the configured drift signals (win-rate + entry-time).

Returns 404 if the version is missing or has no baseline assigned.
The "no live run yet" case is NOT a 404 — it's a valid drift state
surfaced as WARN results so the UI can render the panel.

Return the live ingester's most recent heartbeat.

404 if the file doesn't exist (ingester not running or never run).
422 if the file exists but is malformed.

Snapshot of the live-trades pipeline (DB latest live run + inbox + import log).

Used by the /monitor page to surface silent failures of the daily
Taildrop + import scheduled task — silent because the importer can
log "errors=0" while producing nothing (lesson from the parquet_mirror
schema-mismatch bug, 2026-04-27).

Recent live signals, ordered newest first.

Filters AND together. `strategy_id` resolves to all of that
strategy's version ids and matches LiveSignal.strategy_version_id
against any of them. Used by the Monitor session journal panel to
show today's signals for the currently-live strategy.

parameters.description: ISO datetime; only signals with ts >= since are returned
parameters.name: since

schema.description: ISO datetime; only signals with ts >= since are returned
schema.title: Since
Response List Live Signals Api Monitor Signals Get

get.description:
List notes with optional filters.

Filters AND together. `tag` matches when the value is in the JSON
tags list. `note_type` is validated against NOTE_TYPES.

schema.title: Response List Notes Api Notes Get

get.description: Vocabulary endpoint for note_type. Mirrors STRATEGY_STAGES pattern.
Vocabulary endpoint for the mode picker. Mirrors STRATEGY_STAGES.

Legacy shape for the deterministic single-path checker embedded
on /backtests/[id]. Reads from the DB now (so user edits flow
through), maps each row to the lean PropFirmPreset shape.

schema.title: Response List Presets Api Prop Firm Presets Get

get.description:
List firm rule profiles. Active by default; pass
`?include_archived=true` to include soft-deleted ones too.

schema.title: Response List Firm Profiles Api Prop Firm Profiles Get

post.description:
Create a custom firm profile. `is_seed=False` so it's never
overwritten by the seed-on-empty pass and isn't eligible for
`/reset`.

patch.description:
Partial update. Pydantic v2's `exclude_unset=True` distinguishes
"field omitted" from "field set to null", which matters for
nullable rule fields like `daily_loss_limit`.

post.description:
Restore a seed profile to its `app.services.prop_firm.PRESETS`
factory values. Returns 404 if the profile isn't a seed (user-
created profiles have no factory to revert to).

schema.title: Response List Simulations Api Prop Firm Simulations Get

post.description: Run a Monte Carlo prop-firm simulation against one or more backtests.

get.description:
Return 1m bars for `symbol` on `date` + optional run entries.

`date` is the trading day (UTC, inclusive). The bar window is
`[date 00:00, date+1 00:00)` — same convention as `read_bars`.

schema.title: Response List Profiles Api Risk Profiles Get

get.description: Vocabulary endpoint — same pattern as /strategies/stages.

patch.description: PATCH applies only the fields the caller actually sent.

post.description:
Walk a run's trades through the profile and report violations.

`run_id` is a query parameter so this is a simple POST without a
body (no fields needed). 404 when either the profile or run is
missing.

get.description:
Snapshot of how the app is currently configured.

No persistence yet — every field is derived from process state at
request time. Frontend polls or fetches once at page load.

schema.title: Response List Strategies Api Strategies Get

get.description: Lifecycle vocabulary. Frontend pipeline column order.

delete.description:
Delete a strategy — only allowed when it has zero versions.

This is the "I created the wrong slug, clean it up" path. It will NOT
cascade to delete imported runs + trades + equity + metrics — if a
strategy has versions, we force the user to either delete each
version + its runs explicitly, or archive the strategy instead
(PATCH status="archived").

Returns 409 if the strategy still has versions attached.

schema.title: Response List Chat Messages Api Strategies  Strategy Id  Chat Get

get.description:
All backtest runs across every version of this strategy.

Replaces the frontend pattern of fetching /api/backtests and
filtering client-side — that approach is O(all runs in the system)
which grows poorly.

schema.title: Response List Strategy Runs Api Strategies  Strategy Id  Runs Get

delete.description:
Delete a version — only allowed when it has zero attached runs.

Before this change, SQLAlchemy's cascade="all, delete-orphan" on
StrategyVersion.runs would silently nuke every imported run, trade,
equity point, and metric with the version. Now the endpoint refuses
with 409 when runs exist and points the caller at the archive flow.

patch.description: Mark a version archived. Non-destructive — runs/trades/metrics untouched.

Designate (or clear) the baseline run used by the Forward Drift Monitor.

The baseline is the run we expect live behavior to match. It must be
a non-live run (source="imported" or "engine") — comparing live to
itself is meaningless, so live runs are rejected with 422.

`run_id=null` clears the baseline.

get.description:
List live runs + their trades for the picker.

`tbbo_available` is computed per trade by checking the TBBO partition
on disk. Cheap dir-stat — no parquet read.

schema.title: Response List Live Runs Api Trade Replay Runs Get

get.description:
Return TBBO ticks within the configured window around a trade.

404 if run/trade not found or if the TBBO partition for the trade's
date doesn't exist on disk. The frontend picker should already
prevent this case (it disables trades with `tbbo_available=False`),
but the endpoint enforces it for honesty.
